prompt:
We switched exactOptionalPropertyTypes and noImplicitOverride on for the signage repo this sprint, and the reason for the first one is painfully real: the scheduler diffs panel configs by key presence, and we have been shipping ghost keys that hold undefined, which makes half the fleet look 'configured' when it isn't. The contract is and always was: absent key means unset. Anyway, the fleet module now fails the gate — `bash run_verify.sh`, npm ci first — with:

  signfleet.ts(11,3): error TS2375: Type '{ brightness: number | undefined; caption: string | undefined; }' is not assignable to type 'DisplayConfig' with 'exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true'. Consider adding 'undefined' to the types of the target's properties.
  signfleet.ts(31,5): error TS2412: Type 'number | undefined' is not assignable to type 'number' with 'exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true'. Consider adding 'undefined' to the type of the target.
  signfleet.ts(41,5): error TS2412: Type 'undefined' is not assignable to type 'string' with 'exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true'. Consider adding 'undefined' to the type of the target.
  signfleet.ts(58,3): error TS4114: This member must have an 'override' modifier because it overrides a member in the base class 'Panel'.
  signfleet.ts(62,3): error TS4114: This member must have an 'override' modifier because it overrides a member in the base class 'Panel'.
  signfleet.ts(70,3): error TS4114: This member must have an 'override' modifier because it overrides a member in the base class 'LedBoard'.
  signfleet.ts(74,3): error TS4114: This member must have an 'override' modifier because it overrides a member in the base class 'LedBoard'.
  signfleet.ts(80,3): error TS4114: This member must have an 'override' modifier because it overrides a member in the base class 'Panel'.
  signfleet.ts(84,3): error TS4114: This member must have an 'override' modifier because it overrides a member in the base class 'Panel'.

Heads up: the compiler's 'Consider adding undefined to the target' suggestion is exactly what we DON'T want — bolting `| undefined` onto the optional properties defeats the whole point of the flag, and the acceptance suite (test_signfleet.ts) deliberately pins key-presence semantics: an empty patch must not invent keys, clearing the caption must remove the key outright, buildConfig must only materialize the settings it was actually given. Decide each site honestly instead. The override situation is just missing keywords — the class tree's behavior (describe/wake strings, power-draw numbers) is settled and pinned. tsconfig.json, run_verify.sh, the test file and the package manifests are frozen.
notes: 5W5 item 2: exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noImplicitOverride retrofit on a three-level class tree (Panel -> LedBoard -> TickerBoard, plus EInkPanel). 9 baseline diagnostics, captured verbatim into the prompt: TS2375 (object literal with explicitly-undefined optional props in buildConfig), TS2412 x2 (unconditional patch assign in applyPatch; '= undefined' in clearCaption), TS4114 x6 (missing override on describe/powerDraw/wakeMessage across the subclasses — TickerBoard's come from LedBoard, pinning the middle of the tree). The compiler's own suggestion (widen props to '| undefined') type-checks but CANNOT pass the suite: buildConfig(40) must have keys ['brightness'] only, applyPatch({}) must not invent keys, and clearCaption must delete — so the honest fixes (conditional materialization, guarded assign, delete) are test-forced, not just prompt-requested; the prompt still calls the trap out explicitly to keep it fair. exactOptionalPropertyTypes requires strictNullChecks semantics, so that flag is on too; baseline is otherwise clean under all three. Gotcha discovered at authoring: node 26 strip-only mode rejects TS parameter properties, so Panel uses an explicit id field — relevant for any future ts seed with classes. node --test passes at baseline (no type-check at runtime), tsc gate is the teeth. Domain: signage panel fleet config — csharp-patchbay is audio routing, go-dashfeed is a metrics dashboard; no seed covers display-config class trees, and the seed's substance is optional-vs-undefined discipline. Toolchain observed: node 26.3.0, npm 11.16.0, tsc 5.9.3 (pinned devDependency, npm ci via reference_setup).
